method_atom: an orthogonal decomposition of a local flow/velocity field into a component aligned with a primary directional (momentum-like) quantity and an orthogonal residual encoding internal motion, followed by substitution of the full decomposed field into a kinetic-energy term of an energy or Lagrangian functional, which reorganizes into an effective potential term contributed entirely by the orthogonal internal component; a hydrodynamic reformulation in which an emergent effective potential arises from otherwise-hidden internal degrees of freedom
object_atom: a non-relativistic quantum particle carrying spin, described by the Pauli current, whose motion splits into center-of-mass translation and internal spin motion (Zitterbewegung), reformulated as the Madelung fluid with its associated quantum potential
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the parallel/orthogonal velocity-field decomposition and its reinsertion into a kinetic functional to yield an effective potential from the orthogonal part, while the spin-carrying quantum particle, Zitterbewegung, and Madelung-fluid/quantum-potential framing are the physics-specific object.
